title: All validation passes run at compile() not evaluate() — correct API contract
description: compile() runs three sequential passes: require-extension validation, validate_script (comparator + regex), and build_regex_cache. evaluate() takes only &CompiledScript and &[u8] and returns Vec<SieveAction> — no Result. A reviewer might suggest moving some validation into evaluate() for 'flexibility'. That would be wrong: it would make evaluate() fallible, force callers to handle errors at evaluation time rather than load time, and violate the compile-once-evaluate-many contract. The design is correct. evaluate() must remain infallible.
